prompt: design a solar panel mounting rack

intent.design_goal: Provide a structural solution that securely mounts solar panels on a building or ground platform, allowing for optimal tilt and orientation while meeting safety, durability, and cost criteria.

constraints.description: Must support the weight of the panels and environmental loads (wind, snow, seismic) without excessive deflection or failure.
Should use materials or coatings suitable for the local environment to avoid degradation.
Design should be cost-effective relative to the budget for the installation project.
Must be compatible with the intended mounting platform (roof type, ground, wall) and installation practices.

intent.raw_input: design a solar panel mounting rack

requirement_spec.design_goal: Provide a structural solution that securely mounts solar panels on a building or ground platform, allowing for optimal tilt and orientation while meeting safety, durability, and cost criteria.

requirements.description: Design object type resolved as solar_panel_mounting_rack
requirements.originating_text: design a solar panel mounting rack
downstream_design_consequences: Initializes constraint graph even when numeric targets are absent

requirement_spec.completeness_rationale: All required engineering decisions resolved from prompt.

functional_analysis.primary_function: Provide a structurally reliable and cost-effective mounting rack that securely supports solar panels, allows optimal tilt and orientation, and is compatible with roof, wall, or ground installations while resisting environmental loads and corrosion.

functions.name: load_transmission
functions.description: Transmit the weight of the panels and induced loads to the mounting connection points.

functions.name: structural_resistance
functions.description: Maintain structural integrity against static and dynamic environmental loads (wind, snow, seismic) with acceptable deflection and failure limits.

functions.name: tilt_orientation_control
functions.description: Provide mechanisms to adjust and lock the tilt and azimuth angle of the mounted panels for optimal solar tracking.

functions.name: mounting_connection
functions.description: Create a robust interface to secure the rack to the roof, wall, or ground substrate.

functions.name: corrosion_protection
functions.description: Select and apply materials or coatings that resist corrosion under the local environmental conditions.

functions.name: installation_compatibility
functions.description: Ensure the rack design aligns with standard installation practices for the intended mounting substrate.

functions.name: cost_control
functions.description: Optimize material usage and manufacturing steps to keep the rack within the project budget.

flows.description: Load path data from the transmission system informs structural analysis.
Adjustment data changes the load distribution to the transmission system.
Mounting connection details are used by the installation compatibility evaluator.
Corrosion–resistant material specifications are supplied to the load transmission components.
Corrosion–resistant material specifications are supplied to the anchoring system.
Cost constraint data influences material selection for the load transmission parts.
Cost constraint data influences overall structural design.
Cost constraint data influences the design of the tilt/orientation mechanism.
Cost constraint data influences the anchoring system design.

required_assemblies.name: Panel Mount Interface
required_assemblies.purpose: Attach solar panels and allow adjustment of angle.

required_assemblies.name: Support Frame
required_assemblies.purpose: Provide the main structural backbone of the rack.

required_assemblies.name: Anchoring System
required_assemblies.purpose: Secure the rack to the building or ground substrate.

required_assemblies.name: Corrosion Protection Module
required_assemblies.purpose: Apply corrosion‑resistant coatings or select materials.

required_assemblies.name: Cost Control Subsystem
required_assemblies.purpose: Track and constrain total system cost.
